bug-hunt — Autonomous Unit-Test Writing and Bug Finding

WHAT IT DOES
Continuously writes unit tests to discover bugs. Runs indefinitely without supervision.
Core loop: write test → run tests → if test fails → bug found → record → repeat.
Never fixes bugs. Only finds them.

CORE WORKFLOW
1. setup    — configure test command, framework, scope; generate risk-map.json and recon-report.json
2. loop     — autonomous test-writing loop; uses adaptive strategy to select high-value targets
3. analysis — summarize results, bugs found, coverage, recommendations
4. eval     — optional self-test mode against planted bugs to measure effectiveness

ROUTING
- No bug-hunt.toml → run setup.md (includes recon.md)
- bug-hunt.toml exists + user asks "analysis" → run analysis.md
- bug-hunt.toml exists + user asks "--eval" → run eval.md
- bug-hunt.toml exists → run loop.md (includes verification.md)

KEY FILES
bug-hunt.toml          — configuration (test command, framework, scope, timeouts)
bug-hunt-context.md    — agent's living knowledge base (coverage gaps, known bugs)
risk-map.json          — function-level risk scores (0-10) generated by analysis-engine.md
strategy-state.json    — adaptive test-type weights; updated each iteration
recon-report.json      — tech stack, entry points, trust boundaries (generated by recon.md)
results.tsv            — structured log of every test written and bug found (gitignored)
eval-report.json       — effectiveness metrics from --eval mode

RESULT STATUSES
test-added  — new test passes; coverage increased
bug-found   — new test fails; real bug confirmed (after verification.md check)
crash       — test command failed to run
flaky       — test failed but not reproducible; discarded
by-design   — behavior is intentional; not a bug

SUPPORTED AGENTS
Claude Code, OpenCode, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, and 40+ more via skills CLI.

INSTALL
  npx skills add gpBlockchain/bug-hunt

SOURCE
  https://github.com/gpBlockchain/bug-hunt
